trajectory_summary: This is a warm referral call that built energy steadily through the verify section and peaked when Jacob volunteered his interest in the portability feature. The rep executed the structural beats competently but failed to convert the call's energy peak into a locked appointment or confirmed follow-up time. The call ends in a soft callback — bill pending, no appointment on the calendar, no confirmed time for the follow-up text. Lead type is unconfirmed from the transcript; if this is a paid lead, the call does not pay until the bill is captured and a sit is booked; if organic/referral, booking without the bill was permissible and the non-booking is the primary gap.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is unknown, but the observable outcome is a soft callback, not a booked appointment. Jacob volunteered a specific availability window (3pm EST) that Holland did not convert into a locked follow-up time or a tentative appointment. The call's energy built to a genuine peak at 03:36-03:44 and was not closed on. Lead type metadata is needed per schema context.lead_types_and_bill_collection_rules — if paid lead, this call does not pay; if organic/referral lead, bill can be collected pre-appointment and the non-booking is the primary gap regardless.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Button-up execution on a warm referral lead
primary_coaching_focus.quote: It's in the mail. And I'm driving home from Tennessee right now, so I'll be home around 03:00 EST.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Jacob handed Holland a specific availability window, a referral-validated interest hook, and zero resistance — the full button_up protocol was available and unused. No appointment was booked, no time was locked, Zoe was not included, and the call ended in a soft callback. On a referral lead this warm, the non-booking is the primary gap. Lead type metadata is needed to confirm whether this call pays at all per schema context.lead_types_and_bill_collection_rules — if paid lead, it does not pay; if organic/referral, booking without the bill was permissible and the missed appointment is the only gap.

key_moments.quote: Is there anybody else on the title of the home with you?
key_moments.stewart_read: Holland asks the title question correctly and learns that Zoe Pitts is the owner of record. Jacob clarifies he is on the deed but Zoe's name is on the loan. Holland acknowledges and moves on without addressing the spouse-inclusion implication. Per schema, a co-owner on the title must be included in the appointment. Holland captured the fact but did not act on it — no attempt to include Zoe in the scheduling conversation.

key_moments.quote: how how much would you say you guys are spending on average a month on that bill?
key_moments.stewart_read: Holland captures the bill range ($200-$400) and acknowledges it ('400 is quite expensive') but immediately pivots to the credit qualifier without flipping the bill into emotional alignment against the utility. This is the universal corpus failure pattern: ask + acknowledge + move on. The flip was available — Jacob's $400 ceiling is real pain that could have been turned into the emotional engine of the pitch.

key_moments.quote: So well, one of them is the big one on the left side of the house is gone. The one on the right side like, if you're looking at the house, the one on the right side, we we trimmed down, like, extreme.
key_moments.stewart_read: Jacob is describing active shade remediation — a large tree removed, a second tree trimmed 'extreme.' This is a customer who has already taken physical action to improve solar viability on his property. That is a strong qualification signal and a buying-intent indicator. Holland's response ('Okay. That's cool. Alright.') acknowledges but does not anchor on the signal.

key_moments.quote: he said you guys have the ability to move it as well, which I thought was pretty unique and worth at least a conversation.
key_moments.stewart_read: Jacob is volunteering his specific interest hook — the portability feature — unprompted. This is the call's clearest buying signal and the natural bridge to the appointment: 'That's exactly what our specialist walks through — how the system moves with you if you ever relocate. Let's get you on the calendar.' Holland instead pivots to a feature explanation ('we're a fully in house company') and then to bill collection, walking past the appointment bridge.
